ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An Acoustic Consultant designs and implements solutions to minimize noise pollution and optimize acoustic performance in buildings and public spaces.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Acoustic Consultants work with architects, engineers, and contractors to assess and mitigate noise levels, ensuring compliance with regulations and creating comfortable environments for occupants.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.E./B.Tech in Acoustics or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in science and mathematics can pursue a degree in Acoustics or a related field.
target_degrees: B.E./B.Tech in Acoustics or related field

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a postgraduate diploma or certification in Acoustics to transition into this role.

future_builder.description: Experienced Acoustic Consultants can advance to senior roles or start their own consulting firms.

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Acoustic Consulting by acquiring relevant skills and experience.

progression.senior_level: Senior Acoustic Consultant or Consultant Manager (5+ yrs)
